# Terminal Launcher

> Compose and launch tiled Claude Code sessions with one command. Define terminals once
> as reusable panes, arrange them into a layout, save it as a workspace, and launch the
> whole set — tiled, named, and color-tagged — via native iTerm2 windows on macOS or
> Windows Terminal windows on Windows.

## What it is

- A thin, platform-agnostic Python core (config + composition model) driving a terminal
  backend behind one small interface. No build step to use the CLI.
- Core vocabulary:
  - **Pane** — a terminal identity: `name · color · target dir · model`. Reusable.
  - **Layout** — `single` (1), `split` (2), `combo` (3), `quad` (2x2). `split`/`combo`
    can be flipped horizontally. Empty slots are left as real desktop gaps on launch.
  - **Workspace** — a saved layout with a pane assigned to each slot.
- The pane set is *data, not structure*: any names/targets/colors work. Panes and
  workspaces are user config; the composer and launcher are the product.

## Requirements

- Python 3.10+.
- A terminal backend: iTerm2 on macOS (`brew install --cask iterm2`) or Windows Terminal
  on Windows (`winget install Microsoft.WindowsTerminal`).
- Claude Code (`claude`) on PATH — what each filled pane runs.

## Install

- macOS/Linux: `ln -s "$(pwd)/bin/terminal-launcher" ~/.local/bin/terminal-launcher`
- Windows: run as a module — `py -m terminal_launcher ...`
- Then: `terminal-launcher init` (seed config) and `terminal-launcher new` (compose).

## Commands

`list`, `panes`, `preview <name>`, `launch <name>` (`--dry-run`, `--inject-color`),
`new`, `edit <name>`, `delete <name>`, `pane-new`, `gui`, `init`.

## Config

One JSON file is the source of truth. Resolution order: `--config`/`TERMINAL_LAUNCHER_CONFIG`
→ `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/terminal-launcher/workspaces.json` →
`~/.config/terminal-launcher/workspaces.json`. See `workspaces.example.json`.

## Docs

- README.md — orientation and install.
- docs/concept.md — the canonical concept.
- docs/product/ — product docs; docs/technical/ — code docs; docs/decisions/ — ADRs.
